seoTitle: Old Mill vs Old Station — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Old Mill and Old Station. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Old Mill vs Old Station: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Old Mill and Old Station.
heading: Old Mill vs Old Station — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Old Mill, New South Wales and Old Station, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Old Mill has a lower overall climate risk score (35/100) compared to Old Station (47/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Old Mill or Old Station safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Old Mill has a flood risk score of 24/100, while Old Station scores 32/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Old Mill scores 45/100 for bushfire risk. Old Station scores 55/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Old Mill or Old Station?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Old Mill has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.55% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
